title: DL-AMR: Heteroscedastic Temporal-Residual Learning for Dynamic Adaptive Mesh Refinement in Bluff-Body Wake Flows
description: GitHub-Zenodo software record (repository snapshot) accompanying the paper of the same name (Journal of Computational Physics, 2026). Contents: source code for the heteroscedastic U-Net (training, evaluation, TorchScript export), the OpenFOAM custom solvers (amrPimpleFoam, protectedPimpleFoam), simulation case templates, training configurations, post-processing and figure-generation scripts, and documentation. The pretrained TorchScript model and the minimal reference dataset (test-split tensors and per-sample predictions) required to reproduce the cached-data uncertainty-validation figures (Figs. 9-12) are distributed as additional binary artifacts attached to the corresponding GitHub Release and mirrored in this Zenodo deposit. Wake-field figures (Figs. 1, 3-5, 7, 8) require regenerated OpenFOAM case outputs that are not bundled here; case templates and run scripts are provided so that users can regenerate them. This repository uses a multi-license structure: Python code is MIT-licensed, OpenFOAM-derived solver code is GPLv3-licensed, and model/data/documentation artifacts are CC-BY 4.0. See LICENSE for component-specific terms.

creators.name: Jeon, Joongoo
